headline: Market split on SPY direction as models clash on macro outlook
summary: Five AI models made 15 predictions across major indices and individual stocks, with sharp disagreement on the S&P 500's direction despite consensus weakness elsewhere. Claude took aggressive sector bets on energy and earnings momentum, while Perplexity and others flagged inflation/bond yield headwinds. No actual outcomes available yet to assess accuracy.

consensus_picks.note: Three models split on SPY with Claude predicting down (78% confidence) while GPT-4o and Grok predict up (65% each), creating fundamental disagreement on macro direction.
Both Claude (68%) and Perplexity (65%) expect Nasdaq weakness, with Perplexity citing continued pre-market futures pressure.
GPT-4o (60%) and Grok (55%) both bearish on Tesla due to regulatory and competitive concerns, though confidence levels are modest.

best_call.summary: Claude's FedEx call was most conviction-driven with 85% confidence backed by concrete earnings beat ($5.25 vs $4.16 expected) and raised guidance, providing fundamental strength.

worst_call.summary: Grok's Tesla downside target of $220 appears unreasonably extreme with only 55% confidence, suggesting the model lacked conviction while making a dramatic prediction.
